steps.title: Identify two points on the line from the graph
steps.content: Reading directly from the graph: the line passes through (-12, 0) (where it crosses the x-axis) and (0, -11) (the y-intercept).

steps.title: Calculate the slope
steps.content:
Using the slope formula with $(-12, 0)$ and $(0, -11)$:
$$m = \frac{-11 - 0}{0 - (-12)} = \frac{-11}{12}$$

steps.title: Write the equation and substitute $y = -5$
steps.content:
Using the y-intercept form with slope $m = -\frac{11}{12}$ and y-intercept $-11$:
$$y = -\frac{11}{12}x - 11$$
Substitute $y = -5$ to find $v$:
$$-5 = -\frac{11}{12}v - 11$$
$$6 = -\frac{11}{12}v$$
$$v = 6 \cdot \left(-\frac{12}{11}\right) = -\frac{72}{11}$$
That is the correct answer.
